refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with end-to-end communication latency compatible with safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The candidate FR is already focused on a single functional obligation: ensuring teleoperation latency is compatible with safe remote operation. It does not combine multiple requirements or specify implementation details, thus it is atomic and solution-neutral.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the end-to-end communication latency during teleoperation under agreed network conditions and verify that the 95th percentile latency (p95) is less than or equal to 150 ms, ensuring compliance with safety requirements.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with end-to-end communication latency compatible with safe remote operation.

candidate_dps: Implement a real-time communication protocol optimized for low latency and reliability
Design a dedicated communication channel with prioritized data flow for teleoperation commands and feedback
Incorporate local predictive control algorithms to compensate for communication delays
Use modular communication interface components to isolate network handling from control logic
Apply adaptive data compression and transmission scheduling to maintain latency under varying network conditions
selected_dp.selected_dp: Design a dedicated communication channel with prioritized data flow for teleoperation commands and feedback

potential_sources: Tight integration between communication and control modules
coupling_check.notes: Selecting a dedicated communication channel with prioritized data flow logically separates teleoperation communication from other system functions, reducing coupling and supporting latency requirements.

assumptions_risks: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters to meet latency targets
Prioritization mechanisms do not introduce unintended delays or packet loss
Worksite safety policies are compatible with teleoperation latency constraints
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including local predictive control as a complementary DP to further mitigate latency impact and enhance safety under variable network conditions.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for managing remote control commands and feedback communication

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Bi-directional data flow for sending teleoperation feedback and receiving commands

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Input interface for receiving teleoperation commands

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Physical or logical network interface supporting prioritized communication channel

module_containment_scores.notes: Very broad module; contains DP but with high diffusion as communication specifics spread across submodules.
Vehicle module is focused on physical vehicle systems; communication channel logic would diffuse outside this boundary.
Highly cohesive; communication channel for teleoperation commands and feedback fits naturally here with minimal diffusion.
Safety services focus on alerts and safety monitoring; communication channel for teleoperation is only partially relevant, causing diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The Teleoperation module is the most specific and cohesive boundary to contain the dedicated communication channel for prioritized teleoperation commands and feedback, minimizing diffusion and aligning with functional responsibility.
containment_risks: Potential overlap with Network Link interface management if network protocols are handled outside Teleoperation module.
If safety-related communication prioritization is required, coordination with Safety Services module may be needed, risking some diffusion.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP directly addresses the FR by specifying a dedicated communication channel with prioritized data flow to meet the latency requirement for safe teleoperation, aligning well with the functional requirement.

diffusion_risks: Potential overlap with Network Link interface management if network protocols are handled outside Teleoperation module.
Coordination with Safety Services module may be needed if safety-related communication prioritization is required, risking some diffusion.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The Teleoperation module shows high containment score (9) and low diffusion score (2), indicating it is a highly cohesive and appropriate boundary for the DP implementation. Other modules show higher diffusion and lower containment, supporting this allocation.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of the FR through the IM is feasible by measuring end-to-end communication latency (p95 ≤ 150 ms) under agreed network conditions using the Teleoperation module interfaces.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The Teleoperation module clearly owns the implementation of the communication channel DP, with well-defined interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, Network Link) supporting the functional requirement.

recommended_revisions: Clarify responsibility boundaries between Teleoperation and Network Link modules to avoid diffusion of network protocol management.
Establish coordination mechanisms with Safety Services module if safety-related communication prioritization is required to minimize diffusion risk.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with strong justification and verification feasibility. The Teleoperation module shows high containment and low diffusion, indicating a well-defined and cohesive implementation boundary. Although some diffusion risks exist, they are manageable through clarifying responsibilities and coordination mechanisms, which are recommended revisions rather than blockers. Given the high safety criticality, it is prudent to finalize this level before proceeding further to ensure stability and clarity in the design.

revise.why: Clarify responsibility boundaries between Teleoperation and Network Link modules to avoid diffusion of network protocol management, and establish coordination mechanisms with Safety Services module to minimize diffusion risk related to safety communication prioritization.

assumptions: The Teleoperation module can meet the latency requirement under agreed network conditions.
Coordination with Safety Services and Network Link modules can be effectively established to manage diffusion risks.
open_risks: Potential overlap with Network Link interface management if network protocols are handled outside Teleoperation module.
Coordination with Safety Services module may be needed if safety-related communication prioritization is required, risking some diffusion.
evidence_needed: Verification results measuring end-to-end communication latency (p95 ≤ 150 ms) under agreed network conditions.
Documentation of responsibility boundaries and coordination mechanisms between Teleoperation, Network Link, and Safety Services modules.
